column3: Explore protocol and service architecture for the finalized Fabric implementation baseline: shared resolved bundle view, get_rules/plan_context boundaries, MCP payload evolution, and backward-compatible API shape.
column18: {"id":"E1","status":"completed","findings":"Architecture baseline is already centered on a shared resolver: `loadGetRulesContext()` caches meta/L0/human-lock data and `resolveRulesForPath()` constructs the canonical rules payload consumed by both single-path `fab_get_rules` and batch `planContext`. `get_rules` remains the authoritative single-path API with revision/stale envelope plus audit logging; `plan_context` is the planning-only batch wrapper that dedupes paths and dedupes repeated rule files per entry. Backward compatibility is handled in schemas and matching semantics, not ad hoc branches: legacy meta nodes can omit `activation`, and shared schema preprocessing derives missing `layer`/`topology_type`. Main drift/risk: `fab_plan_context` MCP `outputSchema` still omits `description_stubs` even though the shared resolver and docs/API parity expect them.","key_files":["packages/server/src/services/get-rules.ts","packages/server/src/services/plan-context.ts","packages/server/src/tools/get-rules.ts","packages/server/src/tools/plan-context.ts","packages/server/src/api/rules-context.ts","packages/shared/src/schemas/agents-meta.ts","packages/server/src/services/get-rules.test.ts","packages/server/src/services/plan-context.test.ts","packages/dashboard/src/api/client.ts","docs/initialization.md"],"error":""}

column3: Explore stable-id metadata compilation path: HTML comment carrier, sync-meta extraction, agents.meta schema changes, revision computation, and doctor/lint integration.
Explore ledger path convergence and migration flow: unified path resolver, legacy compatibility, doctor --fix migration, event stream/read path impact, tests/docs/i18n fallout.
column18: {"id":"E3","status":"completed","findings":"Ledger path is still hard-coded to root `.intent-ledger.jsonl` across server, CLI, and docs, despite prior analysis deciding on `.fabric/.intent-ledger.jsonl` plus explicit `doctor --fix` migration. Core seam is `read-ledger.ts`; downstream impact includes SSE/event tailing, HTTP event-store persistence, pre-commit staged-file heuristics, audit/doctor, rehydrate-state, and ledger append. There is no implemented migration/fix flow yet, so compatibility reads, explicit move logic, and coordinated test/docs/i18n/protected-token updates must land together.","key_files":["packages/server/src/services/read-ledger.ts","packages/server/src/services/_shared.ts","packages/server/src/api/events.ts","packages/server/src/http.ts","packages/cli/src/commands/ledger-append.ts","packages/cli/src/commands/pre-commit.ts","packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts","packages/server/src/services/doctor.test.ts","packages/cli/__tests__/ledger-append.test.ts","packages/shared/src/i18n/protected-tokens.ts","docs/getting-started.md","README.md"],"error":""}

column3: Explore tooling knowledge registration and rollout safeguards: manifest source-of-truth, optional JSDoc extractor, regression coverage, execution ordering, and implementation split to minimize conflicts.
